title: Medication follow-up
uae_applicability: DHA depression assessment is directly applicable in Dubai; NICE medication monitoring requires UAE clinician review and local prescribing rules.

source_references.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — depressive features and structured assessment
sections.evidence_summary: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

sections.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations
sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags
sections.evidence_summary: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management, monitoring, and referral
sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

source_references.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations
source_references.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.6–1.3.25 — clinician-led treatment and shared decisions
sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented discussion of psychological and medication options, patient preference, information, monitoring, self-help, referral, and follow-up without autonomous treatment output or doses.

sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.26–1.3.33 — reassessment, risk, and specialist care
sections.evidence_summary: Supports reassessment of prior treatment and concordance, substance use, comorbidity, function, social support, stressors, avoidance, comprehensive risk, side effects, efficacy, and a shared care plan.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad psychiatric medication review, mood stabilisers, psychopharmacology, allergic reactions, laboratory panels, medication levels, and fixed two-to-four-week review remain unsupported.
Medication adjustment, risk conclusions, and all prescribing remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
